data.body:
PASS 1 review (T3 Integration feasibility — overlay): PASS / not-applicable.

The overlay precondition is not met: this plan adopts no new external API/CLI/service/library and asserts no capability it has not used before. The work is purely internal telemetry/guard plumbing across already-existing modules (`batch_dispatch.py`, `applier.py`, `reconcile.py`):
- The sub-op counts being surfaced (`links_applied`/`comments_applied`/`labels_applied`) already exist in the codebase — the inbound applier returns `links_applied` today (apply_inbound.py:469-497; tests in test_applier_inbound_links.py). This story brings the outbound batch path to parity.
- The Jira side-effect calls (`update_one`, `_call_with_retry`) are pre-existing, already-used integrations (apply_outbound.py:154-156), not new endpoints. apply_outbound.py:139-142 already names this very work as the deferred "story E (2359)".
- No new auth/HTTPS surface is introduced; the warn-first flag and canary test are internal.

No external subcommands/endpoints to verify, no capability gap, no SPIKE needed. Empty findings list for this criterion is correct.
